  Scope: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS*.adoc plus RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc.

  Method: find every bold/italic emphasis span (single- and double-asterisk)
  containing a domain concept word. For each hit, classify:

     section (or RBS0's) but the prose uses emphasis instead of {attr_ref}.

     quoin it should reference.

     has no quoin anywhere. Report where it's used and how often; do not
     propose prefixes or anchors — minting is a separate operator decision.

     (*Invocation:*, *Design rationale:*, *Validation errors:* and similar),
     anything inside ---- literal/listing blocks (output templates and wire
     formats are not spec prose), genuine stress emphasis on ordinary words
     (*not*, *every*), and spans that already carry a quoin ref inside the
     emphasis (e.g. *{rbw_depot} lifecycle*).

  Watch for the borderline case where a capitalized concept word rides
  inside a larger emphasized label (e.g. *Heat affiliation:*) — list these
  separately as judgment calls rather than forcing them into a bucket.

  or does it need restructuring (MCM line-break discipline applies to any
  restructured line)?

## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll spawn an Explore agent to survey the emphasis spans across the spec family.

input.description: Survey RBK spec emphasis spans for quoin drift
input.prompt:
Survey the RBK spec family for emphasized words that should be quoin linked-term references instead. This is a read-only analysis — no edits, no commits.

**Scope files:**
- All files matching `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS*.adoc`
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc`

**Step 1 — Collect the mapping sections.**
Read `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc` and extract every attribute reference line in its mapping section (lines between `tag::mapping-section[]` markers, pattern `:prefix_word: <<anchor,...>>`). This is the master quoin registry. Also note any additional mapping sections in other RBS* files.

**Step 2 — Find emphasis spans.**
For each file in scope, grep for single-asterisk (`*word*`) and double-asterisk (`**word**`) patterns in prose lines. Exclude:
- Lines inside `----` literal/listing blocks (output templates, wire formats)
- Lines that are already quoin references (contain `{` curly-brace attr refs)
- Comment lines starting with `//`
- Lines that are just AsciiDoc structural markup (section titles `==`, attribute definitions `:foo:`, list continuation `+`)

**Step 3 — Classify each hit:**

1. **DRIFT** — The concept word has a quoin in the mapping section (check RBS0 first, then the file's own mapping section) but the prose uses raw emphasis instead of `{attr_ref}`. Report: file, approximate line number, current emphasized text, and which quoin attribute it should use.

2. **MINT CANDIDATE** — The emphasized word names a real domain concept (nameplate, depot, reliquary, vessel, hallmark, crucible, sentry, bottle, etc.) that has NO quoin anywhere in the surveyed mapping sections. Report: the word, which files use it emphasized, approximate frequency.

3. **LEGITIMATE** — Leave alone. This includes:
   - Run-in labels: `*Invocation:*`, `*Design rationale:*`, `*Validation errors:*`, `*Note:*`, `*Example:*`, `*Output:*`, `*Returns:*`, `*Parameters:*` and similar
   - Stress emphasis on ordinary words: `*not*`, `*every*`, `*only*`, `*never*`, `*must*`
   - Spans that already wrap a quoin ref: `*{rbw_depot} lifecycle*`
   - Content inside listing blocks

4. **JUDGMENT CALL** — Capitalized concept word inside a larger emphasized label (e.g., `*Heat affiliation:*`, `*Vessel mode:*`). List these separately.

**Deliverable format:**

```
=== DRIFT findings (per file) ===
File: RBSXX-name.adoc
  Line ~N: *current text* → should use {quoin_attr}
  ...

=== MINT CANDIDATES (frequency ranked) ===
Word | Files where emphasized | Count
...

=== JUDGMENT CALLS ===
File ~line: *emphasized label with concept word*

=== SIZING ===
Is the DRIFT bucket mechanical (sed/Haiku-grade find-replace), or does it
require restructuring (MCM line-break discipline would apply to any
restructured line)?
```

Be thorough — read each file fully. The mapping section in RBS0 is the key registry; read it carefully before classifying anything.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my comprehensive report:

## SURVEY REPORT: RBK Spec Family Emphasis Words and Quoin Linking

### STEP 1: MAPPING SECTION EXTRACTION

**Master Quoin Registry (RBS0-SpecTop.adoc)**

The mapping section (`lines 8-581` between `tag::mapping-section[]` markers) defines the complete quoin registry. Key observations:

- `rbtr_` prefix: RB Term Role identities (e.g., `:rbtr_governor:`, `:rbtr_director:`, `:rbtr_retriever:`, `:rbtr_payor:`)
- All domain architecture and operational concepts have dedicated quoin attributes
- No additional mapping sections found in other RBS* or RBRN files — RBS0 is the sole registry

### STEP 2 & 3: EMPHASIS FINDINGS AND CLASSIFICATION

#### DRIFT FINDINGS (1 item)

**File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc`**

```
Line ~365: *governor*
Current text: "about the *governor*, not the member"
Should use: {rbtr_governor}
Quoin definition: :rbtr_governor: <<rbtr_governor,Governor Role>>
```

This is the only instance where a domain role concept word appears emphasized without using its quoin reference. The word "governor" refers to the `Governor Role` (a service account hierarchy member), which has a dedicated quoin and is used throughout the spec as `{rbtr_governor}`.

---

#### MINT CANDIDATES (potential new quoins)

No clear mint candidates were identified. The words appearing emphasized without quoins are either:

1. **Legitimate stress emphasis** on ordinary language: *not*, *built*, *captured*, *fatal*, *soft*, *loud*, *silent*, *rogue*, *pending*, *before*, *as*, *itself*, *caller*, *access*

2. **Technical terms without quoin definitions** (and used consistently in various ways):
   - *convergence* (2 files) — describes a deletion pattern in GAR; appears in RBSAA-ark_abjure.adoc (line 118) and RBSLB-lode_banish.adoc (line 84). This is used descriptively/technically rather than as a named domain role or artifact. Not a candidate for quoining (it's algorithmic behavior, not an entity class).
   - *pending* (3 occurrences in RBSDV-director_vouch.adoc) — describes hallmark state ("image and about present, vouch absent"). Used as a technical status condition, not as a proper noun/entity. Legitimate stress emphasis in context.

3. **Section/document labels** (already quoin-linked or standalone): *Display*, *Source*, *Builder*, *Recipe*, *Leaf shape*, *Architecture*, etc.

4. **Tool names** (italicized for emphasis): *gcrane*, *oras*, *skopeo* — these are external tools, not Recipe Bottle domain concepts; no quoin coverage intended.

---

#### LEGITIMATE EMPHASIS (excluded from action)

The vast majority of emphasis patterns reviewed are legitimate:

- **Run-in labels** (ending in colons): `*Authentication:*`, `*Validation:*`, `*Invocation Pattern:*`, `*Display Results:*`, `*Builder:*`, `*Source:*`, `*Recipe:*`
- **Stress emphasis on ordinary words**: *not*, *every*, *only*, *never*, *must*, *before*, *after*, *itself*, *build*, *access*, *selected*, *fatal*, *loud*, *silent*, *soft*, *rogue*, *superseded*, *rejected*, *byte-identical*, *distributable*, *single*, *artifact*
- **Capitalized concept words inside emphasis labels** (spans wrapping procedure step names): `*Validate Input Parameters*`, `*List Service Accounts*`, `*Synthesize Service Account Email*` — these are procedure steps, not quoin-linked concepts
- **Status/outcome enums**: `**BREACH**`, `**SECURE**`, `**ERROR**`, `**Delete**`, `**Add**`, `**Update**`, `**Retain**`
- **Methodology names**: `**convergence**` (deletion pattern), `**DSSE envelope**`, `**Idempotency**`

---

### STEP 4: SIZING ASSESSMENT

**Is the DRIFT bucket mechanical (sed/Haiku-grade find-replace)?**

**Yes, fully mechanical.** The single DRIFT item is a straightforward find-replace:

```
Current: *governor*
Replace with: {rbtr_governor}
```

No restructuring required. The line context contains no MCM line-break discipline issues; changing the asterisks to curly braces performs the necessary conversion without affecting surrounding text or formatting.

**Command template:**
```bash
sed -i 's/\*governor\*/\{rbtr_governor\}/g' RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc
```

---

### SUMMARY TABLE

| Category | Count | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|
| **DRIFT** | 1 | `*governor*` in RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc:365 → `{rbtr_governor}` |
| **MINT CANDIDATES** | 0 | No domain concepts lacking quoins identified |
| **JUDGMENT CALLS** | 0 | No ambiguous capitalized-concept labels in emphasis |
| **LEGITIMATE EMPHASIS** | ~200+ | Labels, stress emphasis, status enums, procedure steps |

**Overall Drift Saturation**: <1% of emphasized text constitutes DRIFT. The RBS spec family demonstrates strong quoin discipline; the mapping section is comprehensive and well-applied throughout.

content.text:
The Explore agent completed the full survey. Here are the findings:

---

## DRIFT (1 item — the only fix target)

**File:** `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc` ~line 365

| Current | Should be |
|---------|-----------|
| `*governor*` | `{rbtr_governor}` |

Context: `"about the *governor*, not the member"` — the word refers to the Governor Role entity, which has a quoin registered in RBS0 as `:rbtr_governor: <<rbtr_governor,Governor Role>>`.

---

## MINT CANDIDATES — none

No emphasized concept word was found that lacks a quoin. The `*convergence*` pattern (used in RBSAA and one lode file for a deletion algorithm) was the strongest candidate but reads as algorithmic behavior description, not a named entity class.

---

## JUDGMENT CALLS — none

No capitalized concept word embedded in a run-in label was found that required judgment.

---

## LEGITIMATE (≈200+ spans, untouched)

Run-in labels (`*Authentication:*`, `*Invocation Pattern:*`, etc.), stress emphasis on ordinary words (`*not*`, `*only*`, `*never*`), status enums (`**BREACH**`, `**SECURE**`), and procedure step names were all correctly excluded.

---

## Sizing

**Sed/Haiku-grade mechanical.** One find-replace in one file. No restructuring needed, no MCM line-break discipline applies. The RBS spec family is in strong quoin discipline overall — the mapping section is comprehensive and well-applied throughout.

file.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣Bb

## Shape

Pre-MVP source/spec hygiene grown into the ACG audit program, in two strata.
First, targeted allocation sweeps feathered in pace-at-a-time alongside MVP
feature finalization — magic-string -> constant sweeps and noun-cluster
curation, each with a blast radius that merges trivially.
Second, the audit program: per-pair spec/module audits under the ACG move
catalog, run while ACG is young so the guide and the documents harden together.
A seed, not a tree: scope stays tight to the source/spec allocation family.
Resist becoming the junk drawer for unrelated cleanups.

## Governing idea

The authority is ACG (Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/ACG-AllocationCodingGuide.md) —
the spine, the three homes, the move discipline, and the numbered move catalog
all live there.
This paddock cites and never restates
(ACG's statements -> definition-sites clause applied to itself;
an earlier revision of this paddock paraphrased the guide and has been drained).

## Cinched

- ACG = "Allocation Coding Guide" (not "Asciidoc" — the guide governs source
  authoring/allocation; asciidoc is MCM's domain).
  Veiled/proprietary, guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG.
  Not released at MVP.
- v1 posture: bless and name emergent practice; no mandated universals;
  no qualify-time enforcement.
  Universals are candidates confirmed pace-by-pace.
- Audit pace shape: one pace = one spec-subdocument/bash-module pair,
  source-side and spec-side moves applied in one read;
  the inherently cross-document moves (paraphrase collapse, tidemark drain)
  get single corpus-wide sweep paces instead.
- Pilot posture: ACG is untested — three pilots run before any roster of pair
  paces is slated.
  Every pilot's deliverable is twofold: the findings,
  and the ACG amendments where the guide proved vague, wrong, or silent.
  Roster vs further pilots is a post-pilot groom decision.
- Pilot selection (groom survey): the sentry pair tests the edit-time/Palisade
  comment boundary;
  the access-probe pair carries known-positive paraphrase/ornament specimens,
  so the audit proves itself against a known answer;
  the conjure pair is the richest surface and the scale test,
  gated with the rest of the ₣BH lane.
- Pilots execute at top model tier in the operator's chat;
  delegation to lesser tiers is for the post-pilot roster only,
  after ACG hardens.
- Antipattern catalog: mcm_antipattern broadens to cover delivery defects as
  well as document defects;
  Stun, Molehill, and word cancer all home in MCM's Antipatterns section,
  with ACG and the JJK gradient-delivery guidance citing rather than defining.

## Sequencing & lanes

Ungated front, in order: the antipattern-catalog pace, then the sentry pilot,
then the access-probe pilot —
the antipattern pace goes first because the access-probe pilot's word-cancer
findings cite the home it establishes.

The rest of the heat is gated on ₣BH (lode-universal-capture):

- Eviction (removing superseded terms) — ₣BH curates the KILL/KEEP list;
  the standing conformance engine's rows populate per-cluster behind ₣BH's
  cutovers.
  ACG names that engine the canonical home for evicted-term assertions.
- The conjure pilot — its surface is under active ₣BH rewrite;
  findings against it now would go stale.
- The rbrv.env single-home sweeps and the noun-cluster curation —
  their files share ₣BH's rewrite surface.
- Cluster repair (curating living vocabulary to a single home) is disjoint from
  eviction and never removes terms.

Triage rider across all audit work:
Manor / Depot / account-role vocabulary is ₣BZ's lane and
reliquary / Lode / Hallmark vocabulary is ₣BH's — report, never touch.
Verb clusters stay unslated until their owning heats land;
slating against terms two hot heats are still moving would re-create the drift
this heat exists to kill.

## Done looks like

ACG hardened through three audit pilots, amendments landed;
the antipattern catalog homed in MCM with citers repointed;
the rbrv.env literal single-homed in bash and rust as the first worked
ACGm_101 application;
the noun clusters curated to single homes;
the post-pilot groom has decided roster vs further pilots.

## Cinched

file.content:
write re-asserts the same policy and returns HTTP 200.  A re-divest, or a
  divest of an identity already partially torn down, revokes cleanly.

* **Class C only — and why.**  The member-visibility classes (A: forward "does
  not exist", B: backward "is not deleted") cannot fire on a revoke — the member
  being removed is long-established, not freshly created or deleted — so the
  revokes drop them.  Class C (caller-recently-empowered, 403) is different: it is
  about the *governor*, not the member.  A governor freshly re-mantled before a
  teardown has not yet propagated its `roles/owner` to the resource-scope IAM
  cache, so the **resource-scope** revokes (repo and Mason SA) retry the
  `getIamPolicy` on 403 against `RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC`, exactly as the
  grant resource-scope sites do.  **Project-scope** revoke carries none — project
  caches absorb governor empowerment instantly (the grant project sites declare no
  403 tolerance either).  `setIamPolicy` carries no Class C retry: it is gated by

content.text:
Confirmed the finding — and reading it in context changes my recommendation. **I'd hold off slating this one.** Here's why, in layers:

**The single finding reads as stress emphasis, not drift.** Line 365: *"Class C … is about the `*governor*`, not the member."* The asterisks are doing contrastive work — governor *versus* member — the same legitimate stress bucket as `*not*`/`*every*`. The tell: "governor" appears twice more in the very same passage (line 365 "A governor freshly re-mantled…", line 370 "governor empowerment instantly") and both are **plain**. If the prose treated emphasis as the concept-reference mechanism, those would want `{rbtr_governor}` too. They don't — only the contrastive instance got asterisks. The Sonnet survey agent over-read it.

**Even taken at face value, it's the wrong heat.** `governor` is account-role vocabulary. ₣Bb's paddock carries an explicit triage rider:

> Manor / Depot / account-role vocabulary is ₣BZ's lane … — report, never touch. … slating against terms two hot heats are still moving would re-create the drift this heat exists to kill.

So the one and only DRIFT target lands squarely in ₣BZ's (`rbk-14-mvp-citizen-model`) protected lane. Slating it in ₣Bb would do exactly what the rider forbids.

**Net: there's nothing here to slate in ₣Bb.** The survey yielded one finding; it's a misclassification, and were it real it'd be a report-to-₣BZ item, not a ₣Bb pace.

One honest caveat worth weighing: *one* finding across the entire RBS* family is a thin harvest, and it came from a Sonnet Explore agent that I now know misjudged the single hit it did surface. If you expected this survey to seed real work, the conservative read is that it under-counted — the better next move would be a higher-fidelity re-survey (opus-tier, or a focused workflow) before committing a pace, rather than building one on an empty-or-quarantined result.

Want me to re-run the survey at higher fidelity, or note the governor instance as a report-to-₣BZ triage item and leave ₣Bb alone?

message.content: Do rerun at higher fidelity.  I now muse I gave you a bad prompt because I also want to convert plain word use to quoins where appropriate.

content.text:
Good — that's a meaningful scope change: from "emphasis that should be a quoin" to "any prose use, plain or emphasized, that should reference its quoin home." The crux becomes *"where appropriate"* — MCM doesn't want every casual mention of "nameplate" turned into a linked term, or the prose becomes unreadable. So before I fan out a high-fidelity survey, I want to pin the actual criterion from the governing guides rather than let each agent improvise it (that improvisation is exactly what made the Sonnet pass noisy).

Let me load the MCM linked-term discipline and the ACG concept-home rule, plus the full RBS0 registry.

input.description: Size guides and locate RBS0 mapping section

1	<!--
2	Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
3	All rights reserved.
4	SPDX-License-Identifier: LicenseRef-Proprietary
5	-->
6	
7	# Allocation Coding Guide (ACG) — Reference the Home
8	
9	## Purpose
10	
11	ACG codifies one discipline: **don't recreate inline what already has a named
12	home — reference the home.** It is the language-neutral, allocation-facing
13	sibling to BCG (host bash), RCG (host Rust), WSG (Windows transport), and CBG
14	(cloud step). Those govern *how* code in one environment is written; ACG governs
15	*where a fact or a name is allowed to live*, and what source must do when a home
16	already exists: point at it, never restate it.
17	
18	ACG is the **source-side complement to MCM**. MCM builds the named homes — quoins
19	in specs, constants in code; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather
20	than recreate them. That complement is why ACG lives in the CMK kit beside MCM
21	and AXLA rather than in any single-language kit.
22	
23	ACG is **v1**: it blesses and names emergent practice and states the spine. It
24	does **not** mandate universals ("every verb must announce"); those are
25	candidates, confirmed move-by-move, not law. There is no qualify-time enforcement
26	in v1. Every move-type below is licensed precisely — most are detect-and-report,
27	not repair.
28	
29	## How this document is organized — frame, then catalog
30	
31	ACG separates two kinds of knowledge, because they earn their form differently:
32	
33	- **The Frame** (prose, no IDs) — the spine, the three homes, the v1 posture, the
34	  move discipline. Systematic and interderivable; internalized, not cited by
35	  number.
36	- **The Move Catalog** (numbered `ACGm_`) — discrete, verifier-backed move-types,
37	  each of which something *points at*: a constant's breadcrumb comment, a
38	  detect-and-report verb, a conformance-engine row, a review flagging a
39	  violation. An ID earns its existence only when a citer will exist.
40	
41	This split is itself the load-bearing test (BCG Core Philosophy): catalog the
42	discrete moves; systematize the discipline behind them.
43	
44	## The spine — reference the home
45	
46	The governing idea, stated once:
47	
48	> Don't recreate inline what has a named home. **Values → constants; concepts →
49	> quoin-refs; statements → definition sites.** MCM builds the homes; ACG governs
50	> source's duty to reference rather than recreate.
51	
52	This is the **allocation instance of Load-Bearing Complexity** (CLAUDE.md). A
53	recreated value or name is a non-load-bearing duplicate: its existence adds drift
54	risk without adding correctness, and the drift it invites is exactly the gap
55	between intent and behavior the principle forbids. BCG's and RCG's Constant
56	Discipline and Interface Contamination are the per-language instances of this;
57	ACG is the general statement those instantiate, plus the one thing a
58	single-language guide cannot state — *where prose with no code-home actually
59	goes.*
60	
61	## The three homes — allocate by when-read
62	
63	A fact has exactly one right home, fixed by *when it is read*:
64	
65	| When read | Home | Carries |
66	|-----------|------|---------|
67	| **Design-time** | the spec (MCM quoin) | what a thing *is* and why it is shaped so — the conceptual model |
68	| **Edit-time** | the source comment | operational mechanics only — language idiom, and the Palisade (foreign-boundary) characterization a maintainer needs *at the code* |
69	| **Execution-time** | the runtime announcement | what is happening now — intent printed as the operation runs |
70	| **History-time** | the commit | when and why a thing changed — git log and blame own it; version narration in the document body is a tidemark (MCM `mcm_tidemark`) |
71	
72	Most conceptual comments are **temporal misallocation**: design-time or
73	execution-time knowledge dumped into the edit-time medium, where it rots for lack
74	of a forcing function. The spec is re-read when the design is questioned; the
75	announcement is re-read every run; an edit-time comment restating either is read
76	by no one with a reason to correct it when it drifts.
77	
78	Two edit-time/execution-time source-doc forms are distinct and **both blessed**:
79	
80	- **Contract header** — a bounded edit-time comment stating a function's input
81	  contract and invariants. Read when editing the function.
82	- **Intent announcement** — an execution-time printout naming what the operation
83	  is doing. Read when running it.
84	
85	Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-parameter-shows-the-options
86	practice, live in `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil`) is blessed alongside happy-path
87	announcement: an error that lists the valid forms is an execution-time
88	announcement of the contract, not contamination.
89	
90	**The execution-time home is plural off the terminal.** "Announcement" reads as
91	a single printed stream because a CLI gives you one. A routine whose product
92	does not go to a terminal — an MCP command, a library call, a daemon — splits
93	it: the **product** (the value returned to the caller), the **trace**
94	(diagnostic breadcrumbs), and the **error** travel different media, bound per
95	project. Naming the full output-role roster is future work; one invariant earns
96	its place now: **trace has no re-read-every-run forcing function, so it rots like
97	an edit-time comment — never make it the sole home of design-time knowledge.**
98	
99	## The third clause — statements → definition sites
100	
101	The spine governs prose, not only values and names. Every normative
102	idea — a rule, a scope claim, a constraint, a rationale — has exactly one
103	authoritative wording at one definition site. Every other appearance is a
104	**citation**: it may name the home and orient the reader; it may not restate
105	the content in other words.
106	
107	A paraphrase is a fork — a second wording that drifts independently, leaving a
108	reader unable to know which wording is law. A **word cancer** is the metastatic
109	form: variations of one idea scattered across documents, each restatement
110	enlarging the drift surface, none authoritative. The term is deliberately ugly.
111	The pattern is the most recurrent failure mode of LLM-assisted maintenance of
112	large codebases: a model completing "what should be said here" recreates a
113	nearby idea in fresh words, and every recreation seeds the next.
114	
115	For an LLM reader the cost is mechanical, not aesthetic: two near-paraphrases
116	are nearly the same retrieval key, and once they drift the model retrieves a
117	confident *blend* — statement-level interference, the prose analogue of the
118	prefix-collision mechanism in
119	`Memos/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`. A quoin gives a concept
120	one string; this clause gives a claim one wording. Canonical specimen: the
121	2026-06-10 Diff-Friendly-Prose repair, where a context file *characterized* a
122	spec's scope instead of citing it, and the characterization carried the error
123	for months.
124	
125	**Normative register** rides this clause on the spec side. In a spec, every
126	sentence either constrains — an implementer or validator could act differently
127	because it exists — or is explicitly marked as rationale or gestalt (MCM's task
128	lens `**Gestalt**:` field is the blessed form: metaphor marked as interpretive
129	guidance). Unmarked ornament is contamination: it spends reader attention and
130	implies constraint where none exists. A code comment restating design-time
131	knowledge is the same failure in the other medium (temporal misallocation,
132	above); flowery spec prose is its mirror.
133	
134	## The move discipline — one litmus, three rules
135	
136	Every cleanup move obeys one litmus:
137	
138	> **Mutate only where a *wrong* move is cheaply caught; everywhere else the verb
139	> is detect-and-report, never repair.**
140	
141	Code-side moves — the value resolves through a named constant, and shellcheck
142	plus the fast suite verify it — may mutate now. Spec- and document-side moves are
143	detect-and-report until the lexer/linter that would catch a wrong move exists.
144	
145	Three rules ride that litmus:
146	
147	1. **Triage is part of every move, not an afterthought.** Exclude the
148	   false-positive class first; declare the authoritative side before any
149	   alignment; gate scope to settled lanes — never terms a hot heat is still
150	   moving. A sweep run against vocabulary two heats are still relocating only
151	   re-creates the drift it meant to kill.
152	2. **A checker proves itself against a known answer before its output is
153	   trusted.** The conformance fixture (next section) is the canonical home for
154	   such checks — ACG eating its own dog food: a named home, referenced not
155	   recreated.
156	3. **Concept linkage is name-identity.** The implementing symbol *is* the link: a
157	   function name equals its quoin's display-text, a tabtarget filename is the
158	   quoin's sprue, a scoped method is its inlay. A source-to-spec comment is the
159	   *exception*, reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.
160	
161	## The named home — the conformance fixture
162	
163	Rule 2 and move-types ACGm_102/103 all defer to one concrete home: the
164	**`conformance`** fast-tier theurge fixture
165	(`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrn_conformance.rs`), registered in the `fast` suite. It
166	is to evicted vocabulary what BUK's `bug_require_clean_tree` is to the clean-tree
167	gate — the single named place the check lives, so no caller improvises its own.
168	
169	The fixture is a data-driven engine, not a word list. It reads rows of
170	`{kill_stem, keep_contexts}` and scans `Tools/` and `tt/` with identifier-boundary
171	awareness, flagging a stem as a bare token while sparing it inside a kept
172	identifier (`Identifier`) or under an exempt path (`PathPrefix`). A row with empty
173	`keep_contexts` is a *pure corpse* — the term must appear nowhere. The engine
174	proves itself against known inputs before its verdict on the live tree is trusted
175	(move discipline, rule 2), so it ships proven with zero production rows.
176	
177	**The rule: retire a term → add a row there. Never improvise a grep.** Population
178	is deliberately deferred — each term gains its row behind its own cutover, owned
179	by the heat retiring it — never against vocabulary a hot heat is still moving
180	(move discipline, rule 1).
181	
182	## The Move Catalog
183	
184	Each move-type states five things: **Detect** (the rule that finds a site),
185	**Authority** (the ground truth a site is judged against), **Licensing**,
186	**Verifier**, and a countable **Done**. Headers tag the licensing: **🔧
187	mutate-now** (a wrong move is cheaply caught — repair in place) or **🔍
188	detect-only** (read and report; no repair until a verifier exists). Numbered from
189	101 to leave room for insertion; once a move has a citer it is never renumbered.
190	
191	### 🔧 ACGm_101: magic-string → constant
192	
193	- **Detect:** a literal value — a path fragment, a filename, a repeated number or
194	  string — that *constructs or addresses* something, where a named home exists or
195	  should. Not human-facing prose.
196	- **Authority:** the value resolves through a single named constant home (e.g.
197	  the `RBCC_rbr*_file` family in `rbcc_Constants.sh`). The constant is ground
198	  truth; every literal is a copy that can drift.
199	- **Licensing:** mutate-now — the constant either resolves or the verifier fails,
200	  so a wrong move dies loud and immediately.
201	- **Verifier:** bash — `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh` + `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh`
202	  green; Rust — `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
203	- **Done:** every genuine construction site resolves through the constant; the
204	  constant carries a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
205	- **Triage:** naming a file *for a human* in a message is not a magic string —
206	  `doc_params`, `buc_info`/`buh_line` messages, and comments keep the literal.
207	  Exclude that class before sweeping.
208	- **Worked example:** the `rbrv.env` filename sweep is the first worked
209	  application of this move.
210	
211	### 🔍 ACGm_102: name-identity
212	
213	- **Detect:** an operation quoin (voiced `//axvo_procedure` or `//axvo_method`)
214	  whose display-text and implementing symbol disagree.
215	- **Authority:** the implementing symbol is the link (move discipline, rule 3) —
216	  a procedure's function name equals its quoin's display-text; a source-to-spec
217	  comment is the exception where no single symbol can carry it.
218	- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side; no mutation until the lexer/linter
219	  exists.
220	- **Verifier:** none mechanized in v1 — read and reported by hand. The
221	  conformance fixture is the future verifier.
222	- **Done:** a report of operation-quoin / symbol disagreements; nothing mutated.
223	- **Formal dependency:** full mechanical decidability waits on an AXLA arity
224	  change — an explicit symbol lookahead on `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method`
225	  (one `mcm_inlay`, the prefix-disciplined source symbol; mirroring
226	  `axvo_tabtarget`'s literal lookahead). That change carries a migration across
227	  every live operation voicing, so it is deferred to its own pace (see *The
228	  AXLA/MCM interface*). Until it lands, the move is read against the existing
229	  `axvo_*` voicings — which already mark *which* quoins are operations — plus the
230	  implementation symbol named at an operation's detail site (`axhems_scoped_method`).
231	
232	### 🔍 ACGm_103: typed-parameter lookahead
233	
234	- **Detect:** a parameter whose type is resolvable by bounded lookahead but is
235	  not yet carried as a type quoin-ref.
236	- **Authority:** the lookahead scanning rule (below) — the parameter's type is
237	  the attribute-reference the marker reads.
238	- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side.
239	- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter.
240	- **Done:** a report of parameters whose type is derivable but unhomed; nothing
241	  mutated.
242	
243	### 🔍 ACGm_104: conceptual-comment eviction
244	
245	- **Detect:** an edit-time comment carrying design-time knowledge — what a thing
246	  *is*, why it is shaped so, how it relates to other concepts — rather than
247	  operational mechanics or Palisade characterization.
248	- **Authority:** the three-homes table. The spec quoin is the design-time home;
249	  where no quoin exists, the move creates or extends one, then cites it.
250	- **Licensing:** detect-only for the sweep — relocation mutates two documents
251	  (source and spec) and a wrong move is not cheaply caught, so each relocation
252	  lands as its own reviewed move, never batch-applied.
253	- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter plus spec link validation.
254	- **Done:** a report of misallocated comments, each tagged with its receiving
255	  quoin (existing or proposed); relocations land one reviewed move at a time.
256	
257	### 🔍 ACGm_105: paraphrase collapse
258	
259	- **Detect:** the same normative idea worded differently in two or more places —
260	  across specs, context files, handbooks, or comments. The smell: a reader could
261	  ask "which of these is law?"
262	- **Authority:** the statements → definition sites clause. Triage declares one
263	  home authoritative; every other site becomes a citation.
264	- **Licensing:** detect-only — the collapse mutates prose in several documents
265	  at once and judgment selects the surviving wording; never batch-applied.
266	- **Verifier:** none mechanized; recurrence is caught by re-running the detect
267	  sweep against the declared home.
268	- **Done:** for each word cancer: one authoritative wording at one site,
269	  citations elsewhere, the variant wordings gone.
270	
271	### 🔍 ACGm_106: ornament drain
272	
273	- **Detect:** spec sentences in normative position that neither constrain nor
274	  carry marked rationale or gestalt — prose an implementer or validator could
275	  delete without acting differently.
276	- **Authority:** the normative-register rule (the statements clause, above).
277	- **Licensing:** detect-only — whether a sentence constrains is judgment;
278	  draining is per-document and reviewed.
279	- **Verifier:** none in v1.
280	- **Done:** the spec reads as constraint plus marked rationale; deleted ornament
281	  is gone, demoted ornament is marked.
282	
283	### 🔍 ACGm_107: tidemark drain
284	
285	- **Detect:** version commentary in a durable document — revision markers, dated
286	  change narration, "new in vX" annotations. Durable means spec, guide, code, or
287	  context file; a dated artifact (memo, scar, commit message) is exempt — history
288	  is its content.
289	- **Authority:** the history-time row of the three-homes table; the concept home
290	  is MCM's `mcm_tidemark` quoin. The commit is the home — git log and blame carry
291	  the change story with a forcing function the document body lacks.
292	- **Licensing:** detect-only as a sweep; an individual drain is mutate-now when
293	  the deleted text is pure narration (the content survives in git), reviewed when
294	  narration is entangled with normative statements.
295	- **Verifier:** none in v1; candidate for a cheap regex pass in the future linter
296	  (date-shaped tokens and version markers outside memo paths).
297	- **Done:** durable documents read in the present tense; history questions answer
298	  through git.
299	
300	## The AXLA/MCM interface
301	
302	### The lookahead scanning rule
303	
304	When counting a marker's lookahead in an MCM document, scan the following text and
305	**skip prose lines and blank lines; count only attribute-references (`{…}` linked
306	terms) and backtick sprues.** A dimension that counts "the Nth linked term" (e.g.
307	`axd_grouped`) counts attribute-references only — a backtick inlay or sprue is a
308	distinct token type and does not shift that count.
309	
310	### Name-identity's symbol-link dependency (deferred)
311	
312	ACGm_102 becomes mechanically decidable only when an operation voicing carries its
313	implementing symbol explicitly. The surveyed shape: add a one-`mcm_inlay`
314	lookahead to `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method` reading the literal source symbol
315	(the MCM-correct token for a prefix-disciplined name, matching how
316	`axhems_scoped_method` already reads an implementation method name). It is an
317	*amendment* to two established voicings with many live consumers, not a fresh
318	mint, so it carries a real migration and is held for its own deliberate pace
319	rather than folded into guide authoring. Recorded here as ACGm_102's named
320	dependency; raised and deferred, not dropped.
321	
322	## Related Guides
323	
324	- **BCG** — host bash. Constant Discipline and Interface Contamination are the
325	  bash instances of the spine.
326	- **RCG** — host Rust. Constant Discipline (Beyond Strings), String Boundary
327	  Discipline, and Interface Contamination are the Rust instances.
328	- **WSG**, **CBG** — foreign-environment siblings (Windows transport, cloud step).
329	- **MCM** — builds the named homes ACG references; the design-time home.
330	- **AXLA** — the motif vocabulary; the lookahead scanning rule and the
331	  name-identity symbol-link dependency live against it.
332	- **CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline" (mint)** — governs the naming homes
333	  themselves.
334	
335	## Acronym Registry
336	
337	| Term | Expansion |
338	|------|-----------|
339	| ACG | Allocation Coding Guide (this document) |
340	| The spine | "Reference the home" — values → constants, concepts → quoin-refs, statements → definition sites |
341	| Word cancer | Variations of one normative idea scattered as paraphrases, each drifting independently; the metastatic violation of statements → definition sites |
342	| Normative register | Spec-side rule: every sentence constrains or is marked rationale/gestalt; unmarked ornament is contamination |
343	| Tidemark | Version commentary stranded in a durable document; history-time knowledge whose home is the commit. Quoin home: MCM `mcm_tidemark` |
344	| Home | The one right location for a fact, fixed by when it is read (design / edit / execution time) |
345	| Move-type | A catalogued `ACGm_` cleanup move: detect-rule, authority, licensing, verifier, countable Done |
346	| mutate-now | Licensing: a wrong move is cheaply caught, so the verb may repair in place |
347	| detect-only | Licensing: the verb reads and reports; no repair until a verifier exists |
348	| Name-identity | Concept linkage where the implementing symbol *is* the link to its quoin |
349

# Allocation Coding Guide (ACG) — Reference the Home

## Purpose

sibling to BCG (host bash), RCG (host Rust), WSG (Windows transport), and CBG
(cloud step). Those govern *how* code in one environment is written; ACG governs
*where a fact or a name is allowed to live*, and what source must do when a home
already exists: point at it, never restate it.

ACG is the **source-side complement to MCM**. MCM builds the named homes — quoins
in specs, constants in code; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather
than recreate them. That complement is why ACG lives in the CMK kit beside MCM
and AXLA rather than in any single-language kit.

ACG is **v1**: it blesses and names emergent practice and states the spine. It
does **not** mandate universals ("every verb must announce"); those are
candidates, confirmed move-by-move, not law. There is no qualify-time enforcement
in v1. Every move-type below is licensed precisely — most are detect-and-report,
not repair.

## How this document is organized — frame, then catalog

ACG separates two kinds of knowledge, because they earn their form differently:

- **The Frame** (prose, no IDs) — the spine, the three homes, the v1 posture, the
  move discipline. Systematic and interderivable; internalized, not cited by
  number.
- **The Move Catalog** (numbered `ACGm_`) — discrete, verifier-backed move-types,

  detect-and-report verb, a conformance-engine row, a review flagging a
  violation. An ID earns its existence only when a citer will exist.

This split is itself the load-bearing test (BCG Core Philosophy): catalog the
discrete moves; systematize the discipline behind them.

## The spine — reference the home

> source's duty to reference rather than recreate.

risk without adding correctness, and the drift it invites is exactly the gap

Discipline and Interface Contamination are the per-language instances of this;
ACG is the general statement those instantiate, plus the one thing a
single-language guide cannot state — *where prose with no code-home actually
goes.*

## The three homes — allocate by when-read

A fact has exactly one right home, fixed by *when it is read*:

| When read | Home | Carries |
|-----------|------|---------|
| **Design-time** | the spec (MCM quoin) | what a thing *is* and why it is shaped so — the conceptual model |
| **Edit-time** | the source comment | operational mechanics only — language idiom, and the Palisade (foreign-boundary) characterization a maintainer needs *at the code* |
| **Execution-time** | the runtime announcement | what is happening now — intent printed as the operation runs |
| **History-time** | the commit | when and why a thing changed — git log and blame own it; version narration in the document body is a tidemark (MCM `mcm_tidemark`) |

Most conceptual comments are **temporal misallocation**: design-time or
execution-time knowledge dumped into the edit-time medium, where it rots for lack
of a forcing function. The spec is re-read when the design is questioned; the
announcement is re-read every run; an edit-time comment restating either is read

Two edit-time/execution-time source-doc forms are distinct and **both blessed**:

- **Contract header** — a bounded edit-time comment stating a function's input
  contract and invariants. Read when editing the function.
- **Intent announcement** — an execution-time printout naming what the operation
  is doing. Read when running it.

Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-parameter-shows-the-options
practice, live in `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil`) is blessed alongside happy-path
announcement: an error that lists the valid forms is an execution-time
announcement of the contract, not contamination.

**The execution-time home is plural off the terminal.** "Announcement" reads as
a single printed stream because a CLI gives you one. A routine whose product
does not go to a terminal — an MCP command, a library call, a daemon — splits
it: the **product** (the value returned to the caller), the **trace**
(diagnostic breadcrumbs), and the **error** travel different media, bound per
project. Naming the full output-role roster is future work; one invariant earns
its place now: **trace has no re-read-every-run forcing function, so it rots like
an edit-time comment — never make it the sole home of design-time knowledge.**

## The third clause — statements → definition sites

The spine governs prose, not only values and names. Every normative
idea — a rule, a scope claim, a constraint, a rationale — has exactly one
authoritative wording at one definition site. Every other appearance is a
**citation**: it may name the home and orient the reader; it may not restate
the content in other words.

A paraphrase is a fork — a second wording that drifts independently, leaving a
reader unable to know which wording is law. A **word cancer** is the metastatic
form: variations of one idea scattered across documents, each restatement
enlarging the drift surface, none authoritative. The term is deliberately ugly.
The pattern is the most recurrent failure mode of LLM-assisted maintenance of
large codebases: a model completing "what should be said here" recreates a
nearby idea in fresh words, and every recreation seeds the next.

For an LLM reader the cost is mechanical, not aesthetic: two near-paraphrases
are nearly the same retrieval key, and once they drift the model retrieves a
confident *blend* — statement-level interference, the prose analogue of the
prefix-collision mechanism in
`Memos/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`. A quoin gives a concept
one string; this clause gives a claim one wording. Canonical specimen: the
2026-06-10 Diff-Friendly-Prose repair, where a context file *characterized* a
spec's scope instead of citing it, and the characterization carried the error
for months.

**Normative register** rides this clause on the spec side. In a spec, every
sentence either constrains — an implementer or validator could act differently
because it exists — or is explicitly marked as rationale or gestalt (MCM's task
lens `**Gestalt**:` field is the blessed form: metaphor marked as interpretive
guidance). Unmarked ornament is contamination: it spends reader attention and
implies constraint where none exists. A code comment restating design-time
knowledge is the same failure in the other medium (temporal misallocation,
above); flowery spec prose is its mirror.

## The move discipline — one litmus, three rules

Every cleanup move obeys one litmus:

> **Mutate only where a *wrong* move is cheaply caught; everywhere else the verb
> is detect-and-report, never repair.**

Code-side moves — the value resolves through a named constant, and shellcheck
plus the fast suite verify it — may mutate now. Spec- and document-side moves are
detect-and-report until the lexer/linter that would catch a wrong move exists.

Three rules ride that litmus:

1. **Triage is part of every move, not an afterthought.** Exclude the
   false-positive class first; declare the authoritative side before any
   alignment; gate scope to settled lanes — never terms a hot heat is still
   moving. A sweep run against vocabulary two heats are still relocating only
   re-creates the drift it meant to kill.
2. **A checker proves itself against a known answer before its output is
   trusted.** The conformance fixture (next section) is the canonical home for
   such checks — ACG eating its own dog food: a named home, referenced not
   recreated.
3. **Concept linkage is name-identity.** The implementing symbol *is* the link: a
   function name equals its quoin's display-text, a tabtarget filename is the
   quoin's sprue, a scoped method is its inlay. A source-to-spec comment is the
   *exception*, reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.

## The named home — the conformance fixture

Rule 2 and move-types ACGm_102/103 all defer to one concrete home: the
**`conformance`** fast-tier theurge fixture
(`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrn_conformance.rs`), registered in the `fast` suite. It
is to evicted vocabulary what BUK's `bug_require_clean_tree` is to the clean-tree
gate — the single named place the check lives, so no caller improvises its own.

The fixture is a data-driven engine, not a word list. It reads rows of
`{kill_stem, keep_contexts}` and scans `Tools/` and `tt/` with identifier-boundary
awareness, flagging a stem as a bare token while sparing it inside a kept
identifier (`Identifier`) or under an exempt path (`PathPrefix`). A row with empty
`keep_contexts` is a *pure corpse* — the term must appear nowhere. The engine
proves itself against known inputs before its verdict on the live tree is trusted
(move discipline, rule 2), so it ships proven with zero production rows.

**The rule: retire a term → add a row there. Never improvise a grep.** Population
is deliberately deferred — each term gains its row behind its own cutover, owned
by the heat retiring it — never against vocabulary a hot heat is still moving
(move discipline, rule 1).

## The Move Catalog

Each move-type states five things: **Detect** (the rule that finds a site),
**Authority** (the ground truth a site is judged against), **Licensing**,
**Verifier**, and a countable **Done**. Headers tag the licensing: **🔧
mutate-now** (a wrong move is cheaply caught — repair in place) or **🔍
detect-only** (read and report; no repair until a verifier exists). Numbered from
101 to leave room for insertion; once a move has a citer it is never renumbered.

### 🔧 ACGm_101: magic-string → constant

- **Detect:** a literal value — a path fragment, a filename, a repeated number or
  string — that *constructs or addresses* something, where a named home exists or
  should. Not human-facing prose.
- **Authority:** the value resolves through a single named constant home (e.g.
  the `RBCC_rbr*_file` family in `rbcc_Constants.sh`). The constant is ground
  truth; every literal is a copy that can drift.
- **Licensing:** mutate-now — the constant either resolves or the verifier fails,
  so a wrong move dies loud and immediately.
- **Verifier:** bash — `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh` + `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh`
  green; Rust — `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
- **Done:** every genuine construction site resolves through the constant; the
  constant carries a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
- **Triage:** naming a file *for a human* in a message is not a magic string —
  `doc_params`, `buc_info`/`buh_line` messages, and comments keep the literal.
  Exclude that class before sweeping.
- **Worked example:** the `rbrv.env` filename sweep is the first worked
  application of this move.

### 🔍 ACGm_102: name-identity

- **Detect:** an operation quoin (voiced `//axvo_procedure` or `//axvo_method`)
  whose display-text and implementing symbol disagree.
- **Authority:** the implementing symbol is the link (move discipline, rule 3) —
  a procedure's function name equals its quoin's display-text; a source-to-spec
  comment is the exception where no single symbol can carry it.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side; no mutation until the lexer/linter
  exists.
- **Verifier:** none mechanized in v1 — read and reported by hand. The
  conformance fixture is the future verifier.
- **Done:** a report of operation-quoin / symbol disagreements; nothing mutated.
- **Formal dependency:** full mechanical decidability waits on an AXLA arity
  change — an explicit symbol lookahead on `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method`
  (one `mcm_inlay`, the prefix-disciplined source symbol; mirroring
  `axvo_tabtarget`'s literal lookahead). That change carries a migration across
  every live operation voicing, so it is deferred to its own pace (see *The
  AXLA/MCM interface*). Until it lands, the move is read against the existing
  `axvo_*` voicings — which already mark *which* quoins are operations — plus the
  implementation symbol named at an operation's detail site (`axhems_scoped_method`).

### 🔍 ACGm_103: typed-parameter lookahead

- **Detect:** a parameter whose type is resolvable by bounded lookahead but is
  not yet carried as a type quoin-ref.
- **Authority:** the lookahead scanning rule (below) — the parameter's type is
  the attribute-reference the marker reads.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side.
- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter.
- **Done:** a report of parameters whose type is derivable but unhomed; nothing
  mutated.

### 🔍 ACGm_104: conceptual-comment eviction

- **Detect:** an edit-time comment carrying design-time knowledge — what a thing
  *is*, why it is shaped so, how it relates to other concepts — rather than
  operational mechanics or Palisade characterization.
- **Authority:** the three-homes table. The spec quoin is the design-time home;
  where no quoin exists, the move creates or extends one, then cites it.
- **Licensing:** detect-only for the sweep — relocation mutates two documents
  (source and spec) and a wrong move is not cheaply caught, so each relocation
  lands as its own reviewed move, never batch-applied.
- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter plus spec link validation.
- **Done:** a report of misallocated comments, each tagged with its receiving
  quoin (existing or proposed); relocations land one reviewed move at a time.

### 🔍 ACGm_105: paraphrase collapse

- **Detect:** the same normative idea worded differently in two or more places —
  across specs, context files, handbooks, or comments. The smell: a reader could
  ask "which of these is law?"
- **Authority:** the statements → definition sites clause. Triage declares one
  home authoritative; every other site becomes a citation.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — the collapse mutates prose in several documents
  at once and judgment selects the surviving wording; never batch-applied.
- **Verifier:** none mechanized; recurrence is caught by re-running the detect
  sweep against the declared home.
- **Done:** for each word cancer: one authoritative wording at one site,
  citations elsewhere, the variant wordings gone.

### 🔍 ACGm_106: ornament drain

- **Detect:** spec sentences in normative position that neither constrain nor
  carry marked rationale or gestalt — prose an implementer or validator could
  delete without acting differently.
- **Authority:** the normative-register rule (the statements clause, above).
- **Licensing:** detect-only — whether a sentence constrains is judgment;
  draining is per-document and reviewed.
- **Verifier:** none in v1.
- **Done:** the spec reads as constraint plus marked rationale; deleted ornament
  is gone, demoted ornament is marked.

### 🔍 ACGm_107: tidemark drain

- **Detect:** version commentary in a durable document — revision markers, dated
  change narration, "new in vX" annotations. Durable means spec, guide, code, or
  context file; a dated artifact (memo, scar, commit message) is exempt — history
  is its content.
- **Authority:** the history-time row of the three-homes table; the concept home
  is MCM's `mcm_tidemark` quoin. The commit is the home — git log and blame carry
  the change story with a forcing function the document body lacks.
- **Licensing:** detect-only as a sweep; an individual drain is mutate-now when
  the deleted text is pure narration (the content survives in git), reviewed when
  narration is entangled with normative statements.
- **Verifier:** none in v1; candidate for a cheap regex pass in the future linter
  (date-shaped tokens and version markers outside memo paths).
- **Done:** durable documents read in the present tense; history questions answer
  through git.

## The AXLA/MCM interface

### The lookahead scanning rule

When counting a marker's lookahead in an MCM document, scan the following text and
**skip prose lines and blank lines; count only attribute-references (`{…}` linked
terms) and backtick sprues.** A dimension that counts "the Nth linked term" (e.g.
`axd_grouped`) counts attribute-references only — a backtick inlay or sprue is a
distinct token type and does not shift that count.

### Name-identity's symbol-link dependency (deferred)

ACGm_102 becomes mechanically decidable only when an operation voicing carries its
implementing symbol explicitly. The surveyed shape: add a one-`mcm_inlay`
lookahead to `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method` reading the literal source symbol
(the MCM-correct token for a prefix-disciplined name, matching how
`axhems_scoped_method` already reads an implementation method name). It is an
*amendment* to two established voicings with many live consumers, not a fresh
mint, so it carries a real migration and is held for its own deliberate pace
rather than folded into guide authoring. Recorded here as ACGm_102's named
dependency; raised and deferred, not dropped.

## Related Guides

- **BCG** — host bash. Constant Discipline and Interface Contamination are the
  bash instances of the spine.
- **RCG** — host Rust. Constant Discipline (Beyond Strings), String Boundary
  Discipline, and Interface Contamination are the Rust instances.
- **WSG**, **CBG** — foreign-environment siblings (Windows transport, cloud step).
- **MCM** — builds the named homes ACG references; the design-time home.
- **AXLA** — the motif vocabulary; the lookahead scanning rule and the
  name-identity symbol-link dependency live against it.
- **CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline" (mint)** — governs the naming homes
  themselves.

## Acronym Registry

| Term | Expansion |
|------|-----------|
| ACG | Allocation Coding Guide (this document) |
| The spine | "Reference the home" — values → constants, concepts → quoin-refs, statements → definition sites |
| Word cancer | Variations of one normative idea scattered as paraphrases, each drifting independently; the metastatic violation of statements → definition sites |
| Normative register | Spec-side rule: every sentence constrains or is marked rationale/gestalt; unmarked ornament is contamination |
| Tidemark | Version commentary stranded in a durable document; history-time knowledge whose home is the commit. Quoin home: MCM `mcm_tidemark` |
| Home | The one right location for a fact, fixed by when it is read (design / edit / execution time) |
| Move-type | A catalogued `ACGm_` cleanup move: detect-rule, authority, licensing, verifier, countable Done |
| mutate-now | Licensing: a wrong move is cheaply caught, so the verb may repair in place |
| detect-only | Licensing: the verb reads and reports; no repair until a verifier exists |
| Name-identity | Concept linkage where the implementing symbol *is* the link to its quoin |

lastPrompt: Do rerun at higher fidelity.  I now muse I gave you a bad prompt because I also want to convert plain word use to quoins where appropriate.

content.text: ACG is clarifying. Note what it does *not* contain: there's no move-type in the ACGm_101–107 catalog for "plain prose concept-word → `{quoin}` linked-term inside a spec." ACG's "concepts → quoin-refs" is realized in *source* as **name-identity** (rule 3: the symbol *is* the link) and in comments as ACGm_104. The thing you're now asking about — turning a plain prose mention inside the `.adoc` specs into a `{attr_ref}` linked term — is **MCM authoring discipline**, not an ACG move. So the criterion lives in MCM. Let me find it.

962	== Antipatterns
963	
964	[[mcm_antipattern]]
965	{mcm_antipattern}::
966	A named, recurring document defect formally catalogued so that
967	{mcm_curation_action_s}
968	and reviews can cite it precisely.
969	Naming the defect is what makes it detectable and dischargeable;
970	an unnamed defect recurs because no rule can forbid it.
971	
972	[[mcm_tidemark]]
973	{mcm_tidemark}::
974	An
975	{mcm_antipattern}:
976	version commentary embedded in a durable document — revision markers, dated change narration, "new in vX" annotations.
977	The name is the gestalt: the stain showing where the water once stood, saying nothing about where it is.
978	History-time knowledge belongs to the commit;
979	git log and blame carry every line's date and reason with a forcing function, while in-document
980	{mcm_tidemark_s}
981	drift and pollute present-tense reading.
982	A durable document — spec, guide, code, context file — speaks of itself only in the present tense.
983	A dated artifact — memo, scar, commit message — is itself a historical record and may carry revision metadata without fault.
984	Detection and drain moves are catalogued in ACG (ACGm_107).
985	
986	[[mcm_trodden_word]]
987	{mcm_trodden_word}::
988	An
989	{mcm_antipattern}:
990	a persistent name minted from vocabulary worn featureless by ambient software prose —
991	open, close, run, lock, task, manager, handler, grant.
992	The name is the gestalt: a path so heavily walked that no single footprint is findable on it;
993	grep returns a thousand strangers.
994	A
995	{mcm_trodden_word}
996	fails retrieval for the
997	{mcm_digital_mind}
998	and search for the
999	{mcm_editor},
1000	and invites the plan-versus-product bleed that vocabulary isolation exists to prevent.
1001	Semantic fit never rescues it:
1002	the word is disqualified before fit is weighed.
1003	
1004	== Document Elements
1005	
1006	[[mcm_fundamental_text]]

file.content:
== Antipatterns

[[mcm_antipattern]]
{mcm_antipattern}::
A named, recurring document defect formally catalogued so that
{mcm_curation_action_s}
and reviews can cite it precisely.
Naming the defect is what makes it detectable and dischargeable;
an unnamed defect recurs because no rule can forbid it.

[[mcm_tidemark]]
{mcm_tidemark}::
An
{mcm_antipattern}:
version commentary embedded in a durable document — revision markers, dated change narration, "new in vX" annotations.
The name is the gestalt: the stain showing where the water once stood, saying nothing about where it is.
History-time knowledge belongs to the commit;
git log and blame carry every line's date and reason with a forcing function, while in-document
{mcm_tidemark_s}
drift and pollute present-tense reading.
A durable document — spec, guide, code, context file — speaks of itself only in the present tense.
A dated artifact — memo, scar, commit message — is itself a historical record and may carry revision metadata without fault.
Detection and drain moves are catalogued in ACG (ACGm_107).

[[mcm_trodden_word]]
{mcm_trodden_word}::
An
{mcm_antipattern}:
a persistent name minted from vocabulary worn featureless by ambient software prose —
open, close, run, lock, task, manager, handler, grant.
The name is the gestalt: a path so heavily walked that no single footprint is findable on it;
grep returns a thousand strangers.
A
{mcm_trodden_word}
fails retrieval for the
{mcm_digital_mind}
and search for the
{mcm_editor},
and invites the plan-versus-product bleed that vocabulary isolation exists to prevent.
Semantic fit never rescues it:
the word is disqualified before fit is weighed.

== Document Elements

[[mcm_fundamental_text]]

content.text: No frequency rule yet. Let me do one targeted sweep across MCM for any "how often to link" discipline, and check whether RBS0 itself models a convention (does the spec link every "nameplate" or just definitional ones?).

input.command:
echo "=== MCM: any link-frequency / saturation guidance? ===" && grep -niE "first (use|occurrence|reference|mention)|every (occurrence|use|mention|reference)|once per|each time|repeated|saturat|over-?link|sparingl|judicious|wherever|where appropriate|prose mention|plain (use|word|mention)" Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/MCM-MetaConceptModel.adoc

echo ""
echo "=== Empirical: in RBS0 body (after mapping section, line 581+), how is 'nameplate' written? plain vs {ref} ==="
echo "--- plain word 'nameplate' (case-insensitive, body only) ---"
awk 'NR>581' Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc | grep -oniE "nameplate" | wc -l
echo "--- '{...nameplate...}' attr refs in body ---"
awk 'NR>581' Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc | grep -oE "\{[a-z0-9_]*nameplate[a-z0-9_]*\}" | sort | uniq -c
echo ""
echo "=== Is there a quoin whose display text is 'Nameplate'? search mapping section ==="
awk 'NR>=9 && NR<=581' Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc | grep -iE "nameplate|vessel|hallmark|reliquary|crucible|sentry|\bbottle\b|depot|director|retriever|governor|payor" | head -40

content.text:
I dug into the governing guides before re-running, and I found the thing that actually determines the survey's shape — so let me put it in front of you before I fan out, because it changes "where appropriate" from a detail into the whole question.

**The decisive finding: MCM has no link-frequency rule, and RBS0 itself links *sparsely* on purpose.** In RBS0's body, the word "nameplate" appears **26 times plain** and only **5 times as a linked term** (`{at_nameplate}` / `{at_nameplate_s}`) — roughly 5:1. The flagship spec, the registry's own home, deliberately keeps most concept-word mentions plain and reserves the `{attr_ref}` linked term for definitional / first-class / relationship moments. So "convert plain use to quoins" can't mean "link every mention" — that would mean rewriting RBS0's own 26 nameplates, against the convention the spec itself models.

That makes "where appropriate" a real calibration fork, and it's yours to set — it's the difference between a ~10-row survey and a ~1000-row one. Two facts I'll bake in regardless of your answer:
- **Lane triage stays cinched** (from ₣Bb's paddock): account-role (governor/director/retriever/payor) → ₣BZ's lane; reliquary/Lode/Hallmark/Manor/Depot → ₣BH/₣BZ. Those get *reported, never touched*. The thin actionable-for-₣Bb core is nameplate / crucible / sentry / bottle / station / regime.
- **MINT candidates included** — I'm skeptical the first pass's "zero" is right, so the re-survey hunts concept words with no quoin anywhere.

questions.question: How should the re-survey calibrate "where appropriate" for plain-word → quoin conversion? (Emphasis-span drift is surveyed regardless; this sets the plain-prose bar.)

options.label: Structural refs only
options.description: Flag a plain mention only where it does definitional / relationship work, or is a section's first-class introduction, and fails to link home. Matches RBS0's own ~5:1 sparse convention. Smallest, highest-confidence, lowest-noise bucket. (Recommended.)

options.label: First plain use per section
options.description: Flag the first plain occurrence of each concept word in each section. More mechanical and larger; goes somewhat beyond RBS0's current practice but stays bounded.

options.label: Saturate every mention
options.description: Flag every plain occurrence of every quoined concept word. Maximal; contradicts MCM's modeled sparseness and would rewrite RBS0's own plain mentions. Listed for completeness.

content.text: Saturate it is — every plain mention flagged, operator triages. That actually makes this more enumeration than judgment, which means the highest-fidelity method is a disciplined grep/awk pass applied *uniformly* across all files (consistent block/comment/ref exclusions), not 80 separate agent judgments that would each count differently. Let me build the foundation: the full quoin registry and the scope file set.

input.command:
cd /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled
echo "=== Scope files ==="
ls RBS*.adoc RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc 2>/dev/null | wc -l
ls RBS*.adoc RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc 2>/dev/null
echo ""
echo "=== Any mapping sections OUTSIDE RBS0 in scope? ==="
grep -l "tag::mapping-section" RBS*.adoc RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc 2>/dev/null
echo ""
echo "=== Full RBS0 quoin registry: anchor,DisplayText grouped by prefix ==="
awk 'NR>=9 && NR<=581' RBS0-SpecTop.adoc | grep -oE "<<[a-z0-9_]+,[^>]+>>" | sed -E 's/<<([a-z0-9_]+),(.*)>>/\1|\2/' | awk -F'|' '{ split($1,a,"_"); print a[1]"_"a[2]"\t"$2 }' | sort -u

content.text: Full registry in hand: 80 files, RBS0 is the sole mapping section, and the domain vocabulary is large. For a *saturated* count I want one algorithm applied uniformly (consistent listing-block / comment / `{ref}` exclusions), not per-file improvisation. Let me write a single enumerator and run it across all 80 files.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Saturate survey: every plain-prose mention of a quoined RBK concept word,
across the RBS* + RBRN spec family. Uniform exclusions:
  - listing blocks (---- ... ----)
  - // comment lines
  - the RBS0 mapping section (tag::mapping-section .. end::mapping-section)
  - matches inside {attr_refs}, <<xrefs>>, and `code` spans (masked out)
Emphasis (*word* / **word**) occurrences are flagged separately (DRIFT subset).
"""
import re, glob, os, collections

BASE = "Tools/rbk/vov_veiled"
FILES = sorted(glob.glob(f"{BASE}/RBS*.adoc")) + [f"{BASE}/RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc"]

# word -> (quoin, tier, lane)
# tier: T1 distinctive domain noun | T2 generic-but-quoined infra word
# lane: BB actionable-for-Bb | BZ report->citizen-model | BH report->lode | GEN no hot owner
CONCEPTS = {
 "nameplate": ("at_nameplate","T1","BB"),
 "moniker":   ("at_moniker","T1","BB"),
 "bottle":    ("at_bottle","T1","BB"),
 "sentry":    ("at_sentry","T1","BB"),
 "pentacle":  ("rbsc_pentacle","T1","BB"),
 "crucible":  ("rbsc_crucible","T1","BB"),
 "foundry":   ("at_foundry","T1","BB"),
 "enclave":   ("at_enclave","T1","BB"),
 "ifrit":     ("rbsi_ifrit","T1","BB"),
 "quirk":     ("rbsq_quirk","T1","BB"),
 "agile":     ("rbsc_agile","T1","BB"),
 "sessile":   ("rbsc_sessile","T1","BB"),
 "hail":      ("rbsc_hail","T1","BB"),
 "rack":      ("rbsc_rack","T1","BB"),
 "scry":      ("rbsc_scry","T1","BB"),
 "quench":    ("rbsc_quench","T1","BB"),
 "hallmark":  ("rbtga_hallmark","T1","BH"),
 "reliquary": ("rbst_reliquary","T1","BH"),
 "lode":      ("rbtga_lode","T1","BH"),
 "vessel":    ("rbtga_vessel","T1","BH"),
 "ark":       ("rbtga_ark","T1","BH"),
 "touchmark": ("rbtga_touchmark","T1","BH"),
 "director":  ("rbtr_director","T1","BZ"),
 "governor":  ("rbtr_governor","T1","BZ"),
 "retriever": ("rbtr_retriever","T1","BZ"),
 "payor":     ("rbtr_payor","T1","BZ"),
 "mason":     ("rbtr_mason","T1","BZ"),
 "marshal":   ("rbtr_marshal","T1","BZ"),
 "manor":     ("rbtgi_manor","T1","BZ"),
 "depot":     ("rbtgi_depot","T1","BZ"),
 # T2 generic infra
 "operator":    ("at_operator","T2","GEN"),
 "workstation": ("at_workstation","T2","GEN"),
 "image":       ("st_image","T2","GEN"),
 "container":   ("st_container","T2","GEN"),
 "port":        ("st_port","T2","GEN"),
 "volume":      ("st_volume","T2","GEN"),
 "subnet":      ("st_subnet","T2","GEN"),
 "gateway":     ("st_gateway","T2","GEN"),
 "dockerfile":  ("st_dockerfile","T2","GEN"),
 "regime":      ("rbr_regime","T2","GEN"),
}

def mask(line):
    line = re.sub(r"\{[^}]*\}", " ", line)
    line = re.sub(r"<<[^>]*>>", " ", line)
    line = re.sub(r"`[^`]*`", " ", line)
    return line

# emphasis spans on the RAW (pre-mask) line, but only outside code
def emphasis_words(raw):
    # strip code spans so *...* inside ``...`` doesn't count
    s = re.sub(r"`[^`]*`", " ", raw)
    hits = []
    for m in re.finditer(r"(?<!\w)\*{1,2}([^*\n]+?)\*{1,2}(?!\w)", s):
        hits.append(m.group(1))
    return hits

plain = collections.Counter()   # (file, word) -> count
emph  = collections.defaultdict(list)  # (file, word) -> [(lineno, span)]
files_with = collections.defaultdict(set)  # word -> set(files)

for path in FILES:
    fname = os.path.basename(path)
    in_listing = in_mapping = False
    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
        for ln, raw in enumerate(fh, 1):
            line = raw.rstrip("\n"); s = line.strip()
            if "tag::mapping-section" in line: in_mapping=True; continue
            if "end::mapping-section" in line: in_mapping=False; continue
            if in_mapping: continue
            if s == "----": in_listing = not in_listing; continue
            if in_listing: continue
            if s.startswith("//"): continue
            masked = mask(line)
            ews = [w.lower() for w in emphasis_words(line)]
            for word,(quoin,tier,lane) in CONCEPTS.items():
                pat = re.compile(r"(?<![A-Za-z])"+word+r"(?![A-Za-z])", re.IGNORECASE)
                ms = pat.findall(masked)
                if ms:
                    plain[(fname,word)] += len(ms)
                    files_with[word].add(fname)
                # emphasis: any emphasis span on this line containing the word
                for espan_raw in emphasis_words(line):
                    if pat.search(espan_raw):
                        emph[(fname,word)].append((ln, "*"+espan_raw.strip()+"*"))

# ---- OUTPUT ----
print("="*70); print("SECTION A — EMPHASIS (DRIFT) OCCURRENCES"); print("="*70)
if not any(emph.values()):
    print("(none)")
else:
    for (fname,word) in sorted(emph):
        q,tier,lane = CONCEPTS[word]
        for ln,span in emph[(fname,word)]:
            print(f"{fname}:{ln}  [{lane}/{tier}] {word} ({{{q}}})  {span}")

print(); print("="*70); print("SECTION B — PLAIN-PROSE COUNTS by lane / word"); print("="*70)
by_lane = collections.defaultdict(lambda: collections.Counter())
for (fname,word),c in plain.items():
    q,tier,lane = CONCEPTS[word]
    by_lane[lane][word]+=c
for lane in ["BB","BH","BZ","GEN"]:
    tot = sum(by_lane[lane].values())
    print(f"\n--- lane {lane}  (total plain prose mentions: {tot}) ---")
    for word,c in by_lane[lane].most_common():
        q,tier,_=CONCEPTS[word]
        nf = len(files_with[word])
        print(f"  {word:12s} {c:4d}  in {nf:2d} files   {{{q}}} [{tier}]")

print(); print("="*70); print("SECTION C — PER-FILE DENSITY (T1 words, plain prose)"); print("="*70)
perfile = collections.defaultdict(lambda: collections.Counter())
for (fname,word),c in plain.items():
    q,tier,lane=CONCEPTS[word]
    if tier=="T1": perfile[fname][word]+=c
for fname in sorted(perfile, key=lambda f:-sum(perfile[f].values())):
    tot=sum(perfile[fname].values())
    if tot==0: continue
    top=", ".join(f"{w}:{c}" for w,c in perfile[fname].most_common(6))
    print(f"  {fname:34s} {tot:4d}  {top}")

print(); print("="*70); print("SECTION D — TOTALS"); print("="*70)
gt = sum(plain.values())
t1 = sum(c for (f,w),c in plain.items() if CONCEPTS[w][1]=="T1")
t2 = sum(c for (f,w),c in plain.items() if CONCEPTS[w][1]=="T2")
ne = sum(len(v) for v in emph.values())
print(f"  Total plain-prose mentions (all quoined concepts): {gt}")
print(f"    T1 distinctive domain nouns: {t1}")
print(f"    T2 generic infra words:      {t2}")
print(f"  Total emphasis (DRIFT) occurrences: {ne}")
for lane in ["BB","BH","BZ","GEN"]:
    print(f"    lane {lane} plain total: {sum(by_lane[lane].values())}")
